question.text: Which type of resolution is required from shareholders for a listed company to make a preferential issue under SEBI ICDR?

answer.text: No resolution is required if the board approves

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Chapter V of SEBI ICDR (Reg 152-166) mandates that a preferential issue by a listed company requires a special resolution of shareholders. This higher threshold (75% majority) ensures adequate shareholder consent for selective allotments. **The Trap:** An ordinary resolution (simple majority) is insufficient for preferential issues. Since preferential issues dilute existing shareholding selectively, SEBI requires the stronger governance safeguard of a special resolution. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX and NISM Series XII both test the distinction between ordinary and special resolution requirements across different corporate actions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When a listed company announces a preferential issue, check if the special resolution was validly passed — any procedural deficiency can invalidate the entire allotment.
